steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The statement says the student was surprised the Amiga sold "relatively few" units. To justify that surprise, the completion must give the Amiga's correct sales figure and compare it to a system with dramatically higher sales.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 1,600,000 units sold compared to the approximately 18,450,000 units sold of the Atari 2600.: Correctly identifies the Amiga's figure (1,600,000) and pairs it with the highest figure in the table (Atari 2600 at 18,450,000) — more than 11 times higher. This makes the surprise logical. ✓
  • Choice B — 3,935,000 units sold compared to the approximately 4,487,000 units sold of the Apple II.: Uses 3,935,000 for the Amiga, but that figure belongs to the Sega SG-1000/Master System. ✗
  • Choice C — 3,935,000 units sold compared to the approximately 4,487,000 units sold of the Atari 2600.: Wrong figure for the Amiga and wrong figure for the Atari 2600. ✗
  • Choice D — 1,600,000 units sold compared to the approximately 18,450,000 units sold of the Sega SG-1000/Master System.: Correct Amiga figure, but 18,450,000 belongs to the Atari 2600, not the Sega. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
